fitScoreNote: I've spent the last two years doing exactly what this role requires: building production Claude-based agent systems and walking enterprise customers through them in ways that convert skepticism into signed contracts and successful deployments. The through-line in my career is being the person who sits at the exact boundary between what AI can actually do and what the people who need to adopt it are willing to trust. Closing that gap requires both genuine technical depth and the ability to translate it without losing the substance.

coreJobFunction.rationale: Dan's career is a near-direct match for this role: years in technical, customer-facing pre-sales and solutions engineering (Director of Sales Engineering at Smarter Technologies, Customer Engineer and Lead TPM at Thoughtful AI), production experience building agentic AI systems using Claude Code with 23 specialized skills, 5 parallel agents, 57 Python integration scripts, RAG pipelines, and enterprise deployment governance across Claude for Enterprise. As Lead TPM at Thoughtful he directed 1-3 FDEs across up to 18 concurrent customer implementations, owning the customer relationship and translating build progress into business outcomes. The defining superpower: translating complex AI systems to non-technical stakeholders in ways that close enterprise deals, evidenced by a 96% proposal conversion rate and being the sole non-engineering person retained after an 85% RIF.

seniority.rationale: Dan has 8 years of post-college experience with a clear trajectory into technical customer-facing roles that closely mirror the FDE function: Customer Engineer at Thoughtful AI (forward-deployed delivery, leading FDE teams, writing production Python analytics pipelines for customers), Director of Sales Engineering at Smarter Technologies (managing $31.8M in pipeline across 61 enterprise healthcare deals). He meets or exceeds the 3+ year customer-facing technical requirement, has strong Python proficiency, real production Claude and agent experience, and preferred enterprise vertical depth in healthcare.

technicalSkills.rationale: Dan has strong, production-level evidence of Claude-based agent development: 23 AI skills, 5 parallel sub-agents, 57 Python integration scripts, pre/post tool-use hooks, evaluation frameworks, and enterprise deployment governance across Claude for Enterprise. He also built custom Python pipelines per customer deployment to pull blob files from S3, parse agent execution logs, and surface accuracy metrics in Jupyter Notebooks and QuickSight dashboards, which eventually shipped as a core product feature. Python proficiency is well-documented across years of production use. The primary gap is no direct MCP server construction, though the architectural pattern is directly parallel to what he has built.

industryVertical.rationale: The FDE JD explicitly calls out healthcare as a preferred vertical. Dan hits it directly: 2+ years selling and deploying AI automation to enterprise healthcare organizations (health systems, payers, physician groups, dental DSOs), running on-site discovery at Jefferson Health and Presbyterian Health, navigating HIPAA, BAA, FHIR, EDI, and Epic integration conversations with CIOs and IT security leaders. He personally led customer deployments as a forward-deployed engineer at Thoughtful AI, directing FDE teams and writing production analytics pipelines for customer use.

logistics.rationale: Dan is a confirmed San Francisco resident (Richmond neighborhood, since 2020), which is one of Anthropic's listed office locations. The 25% travel requirement is a non-issue given his documented multi-day on-site customer engagements. The $200K-$300K compensation band aligns with senior IC and director-level SF market rates. He holds a BS in Electrical and Computer Engineering from Bucknell, satisfying the bachelor's degree requirement with no clearance, visa, or language blockers.

summary: Led 1-3 FDEs across up to 18 concurrent customer deployments as Lead TPM at Thoughtful AI, owning technical progress and customer relationships throughout
Built and operated a production 23-skill, 5-agent Claude Code platform managing $31.8M in enterprise healthcare pipeline
Wrote custom Python pipelines per customer deployment pulling agent execution logs from S3, evolved into a QuickSight analytics layer shipped as a core product feature
96% proposal conversion rate; only non-Product/Engineering person retained after 85% RIF
Built agent evaluation frameworks: holdout datasets, per-category precision/recall tracking, regression test suites in live production billing workflows

strengths.title: Forward-deployed delivery lead directing FDE teams
strengths.description: As Lead TPM at Thoughtful AI, I was the delivery lead for 1-3 Forward Deployed Engineers across 3-6 concurrent customer implementations, with an upper bound of roughly 18 active workstreams at once. I assigned work, unblocked engineers technically, owned the customer relationship throughout the build, and translated progress and accuracy milestones into terms that meant something to the customer. When something went wrong, I owned the conversation. That is the FDE motion from the delivery lead seat.

strengths.title: Production multi-agent Claude Code platform with evaluation infrastructure
strengths.description: Built a 23-skill, 5-agent Claude Code platform with pre/post tool-use hooks, CI/CD pipelines, and org-level deployment governance, deployed on $31.8M in enterprise healthcare pipeline. Also built the eval framework for a hybrid-RAG eligibility engine: labeled holdout datasets, precision/recall tracking by failure category, and regression test suites for a live billing system where every output had financial consequences.

strengths.title: Customer analytics pipelines shipped as a product feature
strengths.description: Identified that agent execution logs stored as blob files in S3 were useful business intelligence. Started writing custom Python pipelines per deployment to pull, clean, and surface accuracy metrics in Jupyter Notebooks on customer calls. When a particularly technical customer asked for this on a regular cadence, I built QuickSight dashboards against the log data and made public links for customer self-service. Eventually productized the whole pattern directly into the customer portal via iFrame embedding. This was data engineering with no data team: just SQL, Python, and a problem to solve.

strengths.title: Healthcare vertical depth at exactly the enterprise buyer profile FDEs engage
strengths.description: Two-plus years selling and deploying AI automation to health systems, payers, physician groups, behavioral health organizations, and dental DSOs. Ran on-site discovery workshops at Jefferson Health and Presbyterian Health. Navigated HIPAA, BAA, FHIR, EDI, and Epic integration conversations live with CIOs and IT security leaders as gatekeeping decisions for whether deals advanced. This is the preferred vertical the JD names explicitly.

strengths.title: Only non-Product/Engineering person retained after 85% RIF
strengths.description: When Smarter Technologies shut down, I was the only person without a Product or Engineering title among the 15% retained. The reason given explicitly: I can take genuinely complex AI systems and make them legible to C-suite executives in ways that move them from skeptical to convinced. That is the core skill the FDE role sells into the field every day.

weaknesses.title: No formal MCP server construction
weaknesses.description: I have not built MCP servers from scratch. I have designed agent architectures that implement the same protocol primitives MCP formalizes, and my independent research on a healthcare agent data layer is explicitly architected around MCP as the protocol surface.
weaknesses.mitigation: The MCP server pattern: exposing tool primitives over a protocol surface to agent consumers, is exactly the architecture I designed in the Claude Code skill layer. The underlying problem is identical. I am confident I would be building MCP servers within the first few weeks on the job.

weaknesses.title: LLM experience concentrated in the Claude ecosystem
weaknesses.description: My production LLM work is almost entirely Claude-based. I have meaningful exposure to OpenAI embeddings and GPT models from the hybrid-RAG system at Thoughtful, but I have not shipped production systems on top of other model providers at depth.
weaknesses.mitigation: My depth is in Claude, which is an asset for this specific role. The architectural decisions around determinism, eval frameworks, and agent orchestration are framework-agnostic. And the FDE role at Anthropic is specifically about deploying Claude well.

weaknesses.title: Customer-facing delivery formally titled as TPM and SE, not FDE
weaknesses.description: My title was Lead TPM and Director of Sales Engineering, not Forward Deployed Engineer. The work involved directing FDE teams and owning customer delivery outcomes, but I did not carry the FDE title myself.
weaknesses.mitigation: The function was identical. As Lead TPM I was the technical delivery lead who owned customer relationships, assigned work to FDEs, and was accountable to deployment outcomes, not just pipeline metrics. The organizational label was different; the motion was the same.

referralBlurb: I am referring Dan Mathieson for the Forward Deployed Engineer role. At Thoughtful AI, Dan was a delivery lead directing teams of 1-3 FDEs across up to 18 concurrent customer implementations in healthcare, owning the technical relationship with customers and translating build progress and accuracy milestones into business outcomes throughout. He built production Python analytics pipelines per customer deployment that eventually shipped as a core product feature. At Smarter Technologies he built a 23-skill, 5-agent Claude Code platform that ran on $31.8M in enterprise pipeline, and was the only non-Product/Engineering person retained after an 85% headcount reduction specifically because he can make complex AI systems legible to skeptical enterprise decision-makers. His FDE experience is real, just not always titled that way.

projects.name: Personal Website Agent
projects.relevance: Built on the Anthropic SDK with tool use, streaming, and a RAG pipeline grounded in career content. The kind of Claude Code-native agent system FDEs help enterprise customers design and deploy.

projects.name: Healthcare Agent Data Layer
projects.relevance: Full technical architecture for an MCP-native clinical data platform: the protocol surface, audit boundaries, and agent orchestration layer that healthcare enterprise customers are trying to build.

projects.name: Dental Eligibility Intelligence
projects.relevance: Production hybrid-RAG system in a live healthcare billing workflow. Shows the eval framework and determinism-as-design-principle thinking that FDEs apply when deploying AI in high-stakes regulated contexts.
